components.title: DHCP Server (Windows Server DHCP)
components.description: Windows Server DHCP role providing dynamic IP address assignment to workstations, wireless clients, and other network-connected devices. DHCP scopes are configured per network segment (VLAN). DHCP failover is configured between two DHCP servers for high availability. DHCP logging provides a record of IP address assignments for incident investigation.

props.name: ha-mode
props.value: DHCP Failover (Hot Standby or Load Balance) between two servers

protocols.name: dhcp
protocols.title: DHCP — Dynamic IP address assignment to clients

protocols.name: dhcp-failover
protocols.title: DHCP Failover — DHCP server-to-server replication and failover

protocols.name: dns
protocols.title: DNS — Dynamic DNS (DDNS) registration of DHCP leases

protocols.name: ldap
protocols.title: LDAP — Active Directory integration for DHCP authorisation

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for DHCP Server (Windows Server DHCP).

implemented-requirements.description: The Windows DHCP Server role is configured per an approved baseline: DHCP server is authorised in Active Directory (preventing rogue DHCP servers from issuing leases); DHCP audit logging is enabled and logs retained and forwarded to the SIEM; unused DHCP scopes are deactivated; DHCP options are reviewed to ensure they provide only approved gateway, DNS server, and domain search parameters; and DHCP snooping is enabled on network switches to prevent rogue DHCP servers on switch ports.

DHCP server audit logs (lease grants, renewals, expirations, and errors) are forwarded to the SIEM. DHCP logs provide the critical mapping between IP address and MAC address / hostname needed for incident investigation. Retention of DHCP logs aligns with the organisation's minimum log retention policy.
